title: Flavorful Grilled Peruvian Onions Anticuchos
description: A comforting and flavorful Peruvian dish featuring onions grilled to perfection, served in a ramekin. Perfect for a weeknight dinner.

ingredients.name: Cheese
ingredients.notes: such as queso fresco or feta

instructions: Preheat the robata grill to medium-high heat.
Peel and slice the onions into 1-inch pieces.
In a small bowl, mix together the minced garlic and mustard.
Add the olive oil to the bowl and mix until well combined.
Add the sliced onions to the bowl and toss to coat with the garlic-mustard mixture.
Season the onions with salt and black pepper to taste.
Thread the onions onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Brush the grill with olive oil to prevent sticking.
Grill the onions for 3-4 minutes per side, or until they are caramelized and slightly charred.
Transfer the onions to a ramekin and crumble the cheese on top.
Return the ramekin to the grill and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove the ramekin from the grill and garnish with chopped cilantro.
